blocks.text:
In triangle $ABC$, side $a = BC$ and side $b = CA$ meet at vertex $C$, where the included
angle is $C$. Write the area of the triangle in terms of $a$, $b$ and $C$.

blocks.caption: Triangle $ABC$ with included angle $C$ between sides $a$ and $b$.
blocks.source:
point A at=(0,0) label="A"
point B at=(4.5,0) label="B"
point C at=(1.3,2.7) label="C"
polygon points=(0,0);(4.5,0);(1.3,2.7)
label text="a" at=(2.9,1.35) anchor=right
label text="b" at=(0.65,1.35) anchor=left
angle between=A,C,B value="C" r=0.75

meta.solution:
Drop a perpendicular from $B$ to line $AC$; its length is $a\sin C$. Taking $b$ as the
base, the area is
$$\text{Area} = \tfrac{1}{2}\,b\,(a\sin C) = \tfrac{1}{2}\,a\,b\,\sin C.$$
meta.subject: math
meta.title: Area of a Triangle (SAS)
